FAQ - Food Pantry FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS Frequently Asked Questions What do I need to pick up food at the Food Pantry? Be an active student, faculty and staff member and bring your physical or dig

ty and staff member and bring your physical or digital UTEP ID. How can I help support the Food Pantry? Volunteer Donate Monetary Gift What donations are accepted? We accept all non-perishable food an

are accepted? We accept all non-perishable food and hygiene products. Food Items Canned vegetables Canned meat Canned soup Canned beans Canned fruit Shelf stable milk Juice Granola bars Pop tarts Oatm

helf stable milk Juice Granola bars Pop tarts Oatmeal Dry rice Dry spaghetti Dry beans Nuts/trail mix Cereal Pasta sauce Tomato sauce Peanut butter Jelly Pancake mix Hygiene Products Shampoo Condition

lly Pancake mix Hygiene Products Shampoo Conditioner Body wash/bar soap Body lotion Deodorant Toothpaste Pads Tampon How does it work? All food is free for eligible students/employees. Visitors may ch

e for eligible students/employees. Visitors may choose what they would like to take and quantities handed out depend on current availability. Food drives are conducted every semester and donations are

ves are conducted every semester and donations are always welcomed to ensure the pantry is as fully stocked as possible. There are two options for taking food from the pantry. Visitors may take a day'

ing food from the pantry. Visitors may take a day's worth of items (five single serving items) and visit up to twice a week OR they may take an estimated week's worth, and visit up to twice a month. Q

ted week's worth, and visit up to twice a month. Quantities handed out will depend on availability and will fluctuate, pantry staff will hand out items according to availability to keep the pantry sus

s according to availability to keep the pantry sustainable. Who is Eligible? UTEP undergraduate and graduate students who are enrolled in courses in fall or spring semester are eligible to use the Foo

all or spring semester are eligible to use the Food Pantry. Students do not need to be enrolled in summer classes in order to access the food pantry throughout the summer. Students must bring their Mi

hroughout the summer. Students must bring their Miner Gold Card each time they visit the food pantry. Current UTEP faculty and staff are also eligible to use the Food Pantry. Faculty and staff must be
to use the Food Pantry. Faculty and staff must be currently employed in order to access the food pantry. Faculty and Staff must bring their Miner Gold Card each time they visit the food pantry.
